meaning: n.备忘录,便笺,便函,买卖契约书
examples: Perhaps even more so than with letters, memorandum should start with the statement of the purpose early in the message.
When the internal message is lighthearted and relatively unimportant, humor in a memorandum can distinguish its author as a person with personality and depth.
The King exclaimed, turning to the jury, who instantly made a memorandum of the fact.
Memorandum in Support of Ex Parte Petition for Leave to Serve John Doe Summons.
Davis, forwarding the trial records to Root, wrote an introductory memorandum that seethed with indignation.
